firstPrinciple.explanation: Name the bird species, describe the actual view, cite the specific lodge. Generic descriptions are the hallmark of operators who haven't been there.
firstPrinciple.example: Instead of "beautiful wildlife", write "a breeding herd of 200 elephants crossing the Mara River at Governor's Camp".

firstAntiPattern.explanation: Rhetorical questions feel manipulative at this price point. Lead with authority and specificity instead.

firstGoldStandard.context: Itinerary overview — specific, evocative, shows rather than tells, no banned phrases
